purpose: Opt-in bootstrap module for large or niche tools not installed by default; currently installs QEMU full-system and userspace emulation via apt for running non-native OS images and cross-architecture binaries.
invariants: This module is opt-in only — never in DEFAULT_GROUPS; must be activated with --with optional-heavy
Only apt-managed packages are installed here — no version managers, no pipx, no npm
manifest entries use status 'optional' (not 'core') to signal to devtools check that absence is acceptable
gotchas: qemu-system is a large install (~300 MB); running this group on a metered connection is inadvisable
DOSBox-X is NOT installed here — it lives in the default reverse group for scripted legacy-binary analysis
qemu-user-static enables transparent cross-arch binary execution via binfmt_misc — this is a system-wide side effect that persists after bootstrap

key_functions.name: optional_heavy_install
key_functions.summary: Installs qemu-system, qemu-utils, and qemu-user-static via apt, then records them in the manifest

key_functions.name: optional_heavy_record_manifest
key_functions.summary: Upserts manifest entries for qemu-system (binary: qemu-system-x86_64) and qemu-utils (binary: qemu-img) with optional status
